dependencies: Reliance on external service providers for key technology, cloud infrastructure, processing, servicing, and support functions
Dependence on securities exchanges, clearing houses, market makers, dealers, and other financial intermediaries for order execution and settlement
risks: Service providers face technology, operating, business, and economic risks
Interruption or cessation of service by external service providers could disrupt operations
System failures and constraints at intermediaries could result in delays and erroneous execution prices
Switching to alternative service providers may require transition period and result in less efficient operations

regulatory_risks: Extensive regulation under federal, state, and foreign laws by governmental agencies, supervisory authorities, and self-regulatory organizations
Federal Reserve capital requirements and stress testing requirements
FDIC deposit insurance assessments and special assessments
SEC Customer Protection Rule and net capital requirements for broker-dealers
CFTC and NFA net capital requirements
Bank Secrecy Act and USA PATRIOT Act compliance requirements
Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions programs
Community Reinvestment Act requirements
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau oversight
Proposed changes to regulatory capital rules requiring inclusion of AOCI in regulatory capital
Proposed long-term debt requirements for large banking organizations
Proposed amendments to brokered deposits framework
SEC equity market structure proposals including Order Competition Rule and Regulation Best Execution
Department of Labor fiduciary rule changes
Liquidity Coverage Ratio requirements
Net Stable Funding Ratio requirements
Enhanced prudential standards including risk management, liquidity stress testing, and single counterparty credit limits
Market Risk Rule requirements
Volcker Rule restrictions on proprietary trading and hedge fund investments
Potential restrictions on capital distributions and discretionary bonus payments if capital buffers fall below requirements

sanction_risks: Subject to U.S. sanctions programs administered by Office of Foreign Assets Control
Compliance requirements for Anti-Money Laundering and sanctions programs
